req.title: Rate limiting (negative variant)
req.statement: The API shall enforce 60 requests per minute per user; overages shall return 429 with retry-after. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-074 mandates a 60 req/min per-user rate limit with 429+Retry-After on overage. Three orthogonal cases are designed: (1) EP/positive — 59 requests succeed (inside the allowed partition); (2) NEG/negative — 61st request is rejected with 429 and a Retry-After header; (3) BVA/boundary — exactly the 60th request (upper boundary) succeeds while the 61st fails. Each test resets rate-limit state via a dedicated fixture to ensure idempotency. Placeholders mark helper utilities kept outside the 40-line limit.

tests.name: test_requests_within_limit_succeed
preconditions: Authenticated user token available via <fixture: auth_token>
Rate-limit window freshly reset via <fixture: reset_rate_limit>
actions: Send 59 GET /accounts requests sequentially within one minute using the same auth token
Collect HTTP status codes for all responses
trace: R-FIN-074: API shall enforce 60 requests per minute per user
EP: requests in [1,60] partition must succeed

tests.name: test_61st_request_returns_429_with_retry_after
preconditions: Authenticated user token available via <fixture: auth_token>
Rate-limit window freshly reset via <fixture: reset_rate_limit>
actions: Send 60 GET /accounts requests to exhaust the quota
Send a 61st GET /accounts request with the same auth token
Inspect the status code and headers of the 61st response
expected: Response headers contain Retry-After with a positive integer value
trace: R-FIN-074: overages shall return 429 with retry-after
R-FIN-074: negative paths — exceeded limits must be tested
NEG: request beyond quota is an explicit error pathway

tests.name: test_60th_request_succeeds_61st_fails_boundary
preconditions: Authenticated user token available via <fixture: auth_token>
Rate-limit window freshly reset via <fixture: reset_rate_limit>
actions: Send 59 GET /accounts requests to approach the boundary
Send the 60th request (upper boundary value) and capture its status
Send the 61st request (one beyond boundary) and capture its status and Retry-After header
expected: 61st response includes Retry-After header with a positive integer
trace: R-FIN-074: 60 requests per minute per user — boundary at exactly 60
BVA: test at N (60th, last allowed) and N+1 (61st, first rejected)

Each test resets rate-limit state via a dedicated fixture to ensure idempotency. Placeholders mark helper utilities kept outside the 40-line limit.",

      "executable": "import pytest\nimport requests\n\nBASE_URL = \"http://banking-api\"\nENDPOINT = \"/accounts\"\n\n@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)\ndef reset_rate_limit(auth_token):\n    <helper_fn: reset_user_rate_limit>(auth_token)\n    yield\n\ndef test_61st_request_returns_429_with_retry_after(auth_token, reset_rate_limit):\n    headers = {\"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {auth_token}\"}\n    for _ in range(60):\n        requests.get(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, headers=headers, timeout=5)\n    overage = requests.get(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, headers=headers, timeout=5)\n    assert overage.status_code == 429, (\n        f\"Expected 429, got {overage.status_code}\"\n    )\n    retry_after = overage.headers.get(\"Retry-After\")\n    assert retry_after is not None, \"Retry-After header missing\"\n    assert int(retry_after) > 0, f\"Retry-After must be positive, got {retry_after}\""
